Red Bourbon.desc: Brazilian/Cuban heirloom. Naturally sweet with full body.
Red Bourbon.note: Excellent for milk drinks; consistent sweetness.

Bourbon.desc: Heirloom cultivar from Reunion Island. Sweet, balanced.

Yellow Bourbon.desc: Bourbon mutation with yellow cherries. Extra sweetness.

Pink Bourbon.desc: Rare Colombian Bourbon variant. Complex and floral.

Heirloom.desc: Ancient Ethiopian landrace. Diverse genetics, complex.
Heirloom.note: Variable; high acidity; rewards careful dialing.

Ethiopian Heirloom.desc: Ancient Ethiopian landrace. Diverse genetics, complex.
Ethiopian Heirloom.note: Variable; high acidity; rewards careful dialing.

Typica.desc: The original Arabica cultivar. Clean, bright, tea-like.
Typica.note: Prized for clarity and terroir expression.

SL28.desc: Kenyan selection. Wine-forward with intense acidity.

SL34.desc: Kenyan cultivar. Complex with spicy undertones.

Gesha.note: Light roasts preferred; high price; nuanced.

Geisha.note: Light roasts preferred; high price; nuanced.

Caturra.note: Easy to dial; good entry-point for espresso.

Catuai.desc: Caturra x Mundo Novo hybrid. Robust and reliable.

Pacamara.note: Full body; slower extraction; great for milk drinks.

Castillo.note: Clean cup; consistent; good daily espresso.

Mundo Novo.desc: Typica x Bourbon natural hybrid from Brazil.
Mundo Novo.note: Full body; classic Brazilian espresso base.

Robusta.desc: Coffea canephora. Hardy, high caffeine, bitter.
Robusta.note: Used in blends for crema and body. Rarely single-origin.

Liberica.desc: Coffea liberica. Rare, large cherries, smoky.
Liberica.note: Uncommon in specialty. Distinctive jackfruit-like aroma.

Excelsa.desc: Sub-species of Liberica. Tart and fruity.
Excelsa.note: Used in blends for complexity. Rare as single origin.

Washed.desc: Skin removed, fermented in water, washed clean.
Washed.note: Most predictable for dialing; emphasizes origin.

Natural.desc: Whole cherry dried intact for weeks, then hulled.
Natural.note: Heavy body; lower acidity; can be unpredictable.

Honey.desc: Pulp removed but sticky mucilage left during drying.
Honey.note: Milder than natural; more forgiving than washed.

Red Honey.note: Forgiving; good for milk and straight shots.

Yellow Honey.note: Cleaner than other honeys; dial like washed.

White Honey.desc: Minimal mucilage retained. Very close to washed.

Anaerobic.desc: Sealed in oxygen-free tanks. Controlled fermentation.

Anaerobic Washed.desc: Washed process with anaerobic fermentation step.

Carbonic Maceration.note: Smooth body; shorter pulls bring out brightness.

Wet Hulled.desc: Indonesian Giling Basah. Hulled at high moisture.
Wet Hulled.note: Muted acidity; heavy body; acquired taste.

Semi-Washed.note: Between washed and honey in character.

Double Washed.desc: Extended wash cycles for extra clean cup.

Swiss Water Decaf.note: Best decaf method for preserving flavor.
